release_title: Canonical Delegation & Enablement Matrix V2 Release

package_purpose.summary: This package contains the canonical indexes for delegated entities, governed objects, enablement families, surface types, ports, corrected entity roles, and policy-consuming component classifications.
package_purpose.architectural_role: It provides reusable lookup and join indexes required by the responsibility delegation matrix, obligation delegation files, trace coverage registers, rollup indexes, and future PostgreSQL derivation.

source_alignment.release_mapping_law: Responsibility / Obligation -> Primary Delegated Entity -> Supporting Entities -> Governed Objects -> Enablement Family -> Surface Type -> Trace
source_alignment.join_key_law: All index files in this package must preserve canonical IDs suitable for CSV, JSON, graph, and relational derivation.
source_alignment.accountability_law: Assembler Subsystem may remain accountable while execution is delegated to narrower mature architectural carriers.
source_alignment.actor_object_law: Governed objects must not be promoted into actors unless explicitly revised by taxonomy governance.
source_alignment.enablement_law: Enablement is an architectural capability surface, not final code or final method shape.

core_new_or_promoted_governed_artifacts.role: Ingress payload and authoritative runtime carrier

planned_files.purpose: Flat canonical index of all relevant delegated entities, supporting entities, external entities, runtime entities, and classification-only layers used by this release.
JSON equivalent of the canonical entity index with array-native fields for surfaces, enablements, responsibilities, and guard rules.
Flat canonical index of the nine enablement families, their primary planes, purposes, used-by entities, enabled responsibilities, obligations preserved, and anti-collapse rules.
JSON equivalent of the enablement family index with nested arrays for used-by entities, responsibility coverage, required capability surfaces, obligations, and anti-collapse rules.
Canonical surface type index covering Administrative, Managerial, Consuming, Boundary, Contractual, and Observability surfaces.
JSON equivalent of the canonical surface type index with semantic definitions and downstream usage notes.
Canonical index of governed objects and artifacts, including carrier objects, state artifacts, diagnostic artifacts, queue/slot objects, and actor-status guards.
JSON equivalent of the governed object index with explicit actor guards, preservation rules, and must-not-do rules.
Dedicated index of the four corrected port/contract-side surfaces: Envelope Assembler, Assembler Ingress Port, Administrative / Ecosystem Port, and Registry / Delivery Port.
JSON equivalent of the port/contract surface index with direction, payload classes, responsibilities, enablements, and must-not-do constraints.
Canonical index of policy-consuming components and explicit exclusions, especially the Metadata Injector exclusion from policy hosting.
JSON equivalent of the policy-consuming component index, preserving included components, excluded components, and mechanism-hosting rules.
Many-to-many crosswalk between entities and enablement families, including usage role, responsibility context, and exclusion/guard notes.
JSON equivalent of the entity-enablements many-to-many crosswalk.

id_stability: All IDs must remain stable across CSV and JSON representations.
Display names may change only if canonical IDs remain unchanged.
count_validation: The package must preserve exactly 9 canonical enablement families.
The package must preserve exactly 6 canonical surface types.
The package must preserve exactly 4 new or promoted delegated entities.
The package must preserve exactly 3 new or promoted governed artifacts.
actor_object_validation: Governed artifacts with actor_status=not_actor must not appear as primary delegated entities.
Log Level Envelope, Delivery-Cycle Flag Set, and Dispatcher Feedback Report must remain governed artifacts unless explicitly promoted by a later taxonomy revision.
classification_validation: Queue Runtime Layer must not appear as a primary delegated entity.
Queue Runtime Layer may appear only as a classification-only reference.
policy_host_validation: Metadata Injector must not be assigned EN-FAM-002.
Policy-consuming component count must remain 7 unless revised by governance.
surface_validation: Each entity index row must include at least one applicable surface or an explicit classification-only status.
Each enablement family must include a primary plane.
derivation_validation: CSV and JSON pairs must preserve equivalent records.
Array fields in JSON must not be collapsed into lossy strings.
CSV multi-value fields may use semicolon-separated canonical IDs.

examples: Which responsibilities are carried by Dispatcher?
Which port exposes outbound registry contract execution?
Which responsibilities depend on Feedback / Reporting Enablement?
Which enablement families protect boundary exclusions?
Which objects are governed artifacts and not actors?
Which responsibilities govern Log Level Envelope?
Why is Metadata Injector excluded from policy hosting?

downstream_consumers.usage: Uses entity, governed object, surface, and enablement indexes for responsibility delegation rows.
Uses entity and governed object indexes for obligation-to-entity and sub-responsibility mappings.
Uses entity and enablement indexes to prove gap, contract, and doctrine coverage.
Uses all index files for cross-bundle joins, query patterns, and package-wide count validation.
May derive normalized tables, lookup tables, and many-to-many crosswalk tables from these indexes.

non_goals: This package does not define final implementation packages.
This package does not define code classes or methods.
This package does not define final PostgreSQL physical tables.
This package does not define runtime algorithms.
This package does not replace the canonical reference Markdown files.

allowed_changes_without_migration: Adding validation notes that do not alter canonical IDs
